ratchet_ledger.reason: ADR-068 arbitration: ADR-067 dispatch-tiers content legitimately adds worker-dispatch / model-tier / explicit-invocation language to nav-plan, nav-audit, shape-build and AGENTS.md. Conscious bump; to be ratcheted back down when build-codex learns to lower/strip Claude-only dispatch semantics (codex-compatibility Phases 1-5).

Sonnet exec batch P1+P5 (blueprints/plans/2026-07-13-sonnet-exec-batch.md): ADR-069 lands nav:compose's new authoring-failure-modes.md reference doc, which cites the existing 'inject -> check' sub-agent hand-off term once as a Leading-Word example (worker_dispatch capability, not a new dispatch protocol). ADR-070 lands the new reflect:park skill with disable-model-invocation: true (explicit_invocation_only capability), consistent with every other summoned-only reflect member. Both are new generated files (baseline 0 -> 1), not growth in an existing file. Conscious bump; ratchets back down once build-codex learns to lower/strip these Claude-only capabilities (codex-compatibility Phases 1-5).

invocation-direction-law (W5) inventory (ADR-072): root CLAUDE.md gains a new Authoring-conventions bullet ('Invocation category is visible, not just in frontmatter') that names the existing disable-model-invocation frontmatter flag a second time in prose, alongside ADR-065's existing mention in the same file. No new capability — the same flag already counted once, now referenced twice in explanatory text. Conscious bump; ratchets back down once build-codex learns to lower/strip these Claude-only capabilities (codex-compatibility Phases 1-5).

ADR-081 extends ADR-067's dispatch-tiers convention with a pre-dispatch proposal gate, borrowing ADR-040's batch-proposal-gate pattern by name — root CLAUDE.md's dispatch-tiers bullet names 'AskUserQuestion-style ask' a second time, alongside the existing nav:plan Stage-4 mention already counted once. No new capability — the same interactive_choice mechanism, referenced twice in explanatory text. Conscious bump; ratchets back down once build-codex learns to lower/strip these Claude-only capabilities (codex-compatibility Phases 1-5).

codex-compatibility Phase 1 (blueprints/plans/2026-07-13-codex-compatibility.md): scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs extracts a real Codex compiler from build-codex.mjs and lowers four capabilities out of generated output entirely. lowerFrontmatter strips `model: sonnet` / `disable-model-invocation: true` from frontmatter and injects an equivalent Codex-side body instruction (an explicit-invocation-only paragraph / a mechanical-tier delegation note) so the intent survives. lowerWorkerProse replaces the exact worker-dispatch constructs (`Agent` calls, `subagent_type=Explore`, `subagent_type=general-purpose`, hyphenated `sub-agent(s)`) with Codex worker vocabulary (explorer/executor worker), plus the remaining prose mentions of the two stripped frontmatter fields. lowerProjectGuidance softens the `/init`-assumption (`Claude Code's built-in /init` -> generic 'host's built-in architecture-enrichment command' phrasing) so project_guidance carries no Claude-only command claim. All four categories ratchet to zero across every generated file (skills + AGENTS.md) — ZERO, not just lower, because both frontmatter fields are structurally removed and every prose mention found by grep was covered by a named rule. browser_verify, ask_user_question, and session_open_awareness are untouched (Phases 3-4) and keep their prior baselines unchanged.
ratchet_ledger.note: reflect-summarize's prior disable_model_invocation/model_sonnet baseline entries (ADR-079 retired that skill before this ratchet) were removed as stale rather than dropped-to-zero.

ratchet_ledger.reason: codex-compatibility Phase 3 ports the interactive supervision gate without changing frozen Claude sources. lowerInteractiveChoiceProse replaces exact AskUserQuestion prose constructs in skill, bundled-text, and shared-guidance profiles; injectInteractiveChoiceContract hard-fails on anchor drift and adds the single canonical request_user_input/direct-question fallback contract to all nine current choice consumers. Source-owned labels, routing consequences, recommendations, opt-outs, one-shot behavior, and explicit-invocation semantics remain in their original offer sections. The denylist rule remains active for future regressions while all generated counts ratchet to zero.
